lede: A look at September 3 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Tanitoluwa Adewumi and Jérôme Boateng, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: North Korea
events.desc: North Korea conducts its sixth and most powerful nuclear test.

events.title: Rhodesian Bush War
events.desc: During the Rhodesian Bush War a group of ZIPRA guerrillas shot down civilian Vickers Viscount aircraft (Air Rhodesia Flight 825) with a Soviet-made SAM Strela-2; of 56 passengers and crew 38 people died in crash, 10 were massacred by the guerrillas at the site.

events.title: The Holocaust
events.desc: The Holocaust: Karl Fritzsch, deputy camp commandant of the Auschwitz concentration camp, experiments with the use of Zyklon B in the gassing of Soviet POWs.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: France, the United Kingdom, New Zealand and Australia declare war on Germany after the invasion of Poland, forming the Allied nations. The Viceroy of India also declares war, but without consulting the provincial legislatures.

events.title: Siege of the British Residency in Kabul
events.desc: Siege of the British Residency in Kabul: British envoy Sir Louis Cavagnari and 72 men of the Guides are massacred by Afghan troops while defending the British Residency in Kabul. Their heroism and loyalty became famous and revered throughout the British Empire.

events.title: Sinking of SS Princess Alice
events.desc: Over 640 die when the crowded pleasure boat Princess Alice collides with the Bywell Castle in the River Thames.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: During the Battle of Cooch's Bridge, the Flag of the United States is flown in battle for the first time.

events.title: Royal Exchange, London
events.desc: The Royal Exchange burns down in the Great Fire of London.

events.title: Oliver Cromwell
events.desc: The death of Oliver Cromwell; Richard Cromwell becomes Lord Protector of England.

events.title: Treaty of Selymbria
events.desc: The Treaty of Selymbria is concluded between the Ottoman Empire and the Republic of Venice.

births.name: Ray Groom
births.line: Australian footballer, lawyer, and politician, 39th Premier of Tasmania

deaths.text: John Montgomery (Continental Congress), American merchant and politician
Oliver Cromwell, English general and politician, Lord Protector of England

observances.text: China's victory over Japan commemoration related observances: Armed Forces Day (Republic of China)
China's victory over Japan commemoration related observances: V-J Day (People's Republic of China)
